name: Margaret Chen
bio: I have been working in elder care for over 18 years, with the last 8 years focused specifically on dementia and Alzheimer's care. I believe in meeting people where they are — some days are harder than others, and my job is to make those days feel a little lighter. I am calm by nature, a good listener, and I never rush the people I care for. I have worked with several families where the care recipient was very private and I always respect personal boundaries. I am a non-smoker and I love animals — I currently have a dog at home.
prior_experience: 8 years at Sunrise Senior Living as a dementia care specialist
5 years private home care for two separate Alzheimer's families
3 years companion care for post-stroke elderly clients
2 years care coordination support at a memory care unit
certifications: Alzheimer's Association — Essentials of Dementia Care

name: Patricia Williams
bio: I have 14 years of experience caring for elderly individuals in their homes. I am warm and patient, and I genuinely enjoy the relationships I build with the families I work for. I am comfortable with both active and quiet seniors — I follow the lead of the person I am caring for. I am bilingual in English and Spanish which has been helpful with several of my families. I am a non-smoker, very punctual, and I have a clean driving record if transportation is ever needed.
prior_experience: 6 years private home care for elderly couple (one with early dementia)
5 years at Home Instead Senior Care
3 years caring for post-surgical recovery clients

name: James Okonkwo
bio: I spent 12 years working as a certified nursing assistant in hospital and rehabilitation settings before transitioning to private home care 6 years ago. I bring a clinical eye and a warm heart — I know how to identify when something is medically concerning and when to escalate. I work well with families who want a helper who can be a calm, steady presence. I am a non-smoker. I do not have pets but I am comfortable around animals.
prior_experience: 12 years as CNA at St. Mary's Hospital rehabilitation unit
4 years private care for Parkinson's client
2 years home care for stroke recovery client
certifications: Dementia Care Specialist training — Teepa Snow's Positive Approach

name: Rosa Mendez
bio: I have dedicated 22 years of my life to caring for elderly people in their homes. For me this is not a job — it is a calling. I have seen families go through the hardest moments of their lives and I have learned that what they need more than anything is someone they can trust. I am very calm, very private myself, and I do not believe in chatting all day. I believe in being present. I love animals and I have cared for several clients who had dogs and cats. I am a non-smoker.
skills: Cognitive engagement activities for dementia clients
prior_experience: 10 years as primary caregiver for two separate dementia clients (private)
7 years at Comfort Keepers senior care agency
5 years live-in care for Alzheimer's client — advanced stage
certifications: Certified Alzheimer's Disease and Dementia Care Trainer (CADDCT)

name: Linda Kowalski
bio: My background is in palliative and end-of-life care, which has shaped how I approach every care situation — with deep respect for the dignity and comfort of the person I am caring for. I have worked in both hospice settings and private homes. I am trained to recognise and respond to pain signals, changes in cognition, and emotional distress. I am a non-smoker. I am fine with pets.
skills: Meal preparation — including modified texture diets
prior_experience: 8 years at VITAS Hospice as a care aide
5 years private palliative care for cancer clients
3 years companion care for advanced dementia client at home
certifications: Certified Hospice and Palliative Nursing Assistant (CHPNA)

name: Samuel Park
bio: I have 5 years of experience working with adults with developmental disabilities and special needs, and I recently began expanding into elderly care. I am energetic, very communicative, and I love making care fun and engaging. I bring lots of activity ideas and I love to talk and connect with the people I work with. I am a non-smoker. I am allergic to dogs.
prior_experience: 5 years at Community Living BC supporting adults with developmental disabilities
6 months elderly companion care (recent transition)

name: Donna Mitchell
bio: I have been a companion care helper for 15 years and I genuinely love my work. I am quiet, calm, and I take my cues from the person I am with. I have worked with many clients who value their privacy and independence and I always respect that. I am great with routines — I know how important consistency is especially for clients with memory changes. I am a non-smoker and I have a dog of my own so I am very comfortable around animals.
prior_experience: 15 years private companion care across 6 long-term client families
3 years supporting clients with early dementia specifically

name: Grace Thompson
bio: I have been doing home care for about 10 years now, mostly with elderly clients. I am comfortable with dementia care and I have supported a few clients with memory issues over the years. I am a non-smoker and I am good with animals. I am a calm and steady person.
prior_experience: 10 years private home care — multiple clients
Supported two clients with memory issues

name: Michael Torres
bio: I am a certified home health aide with 7 years of experience. I have worked with elderly clients in home settings and I am comfortable with a wide range of care needs including cognitive decline. I am a non-smoker and I enjoy working with families.
prior_experience: 7 years home health aide — various clients through agency
Some dementia care experience

name: Amara Osei
bio: I have 9 years of experience in home-based elderly care. I am known among my clients' families for being reliable, warm, and proactive. I notice things before they become problems. I have significant experience supporting clients with dementia and I understand the importance of routine and calm in those situations. I am a non-smoker.
prior_experience: 9 years private home care
Primary caregiver for two dementia clients over 4 years combined
certifications: Dementia Care certification — Teepa Snow methodology

name: Catherine Lee
bio: I am a certified home health aide and dementia care specialist. I have been working in elder care for several years and I hold multiple certifications. I am passionate about providing dignified, compassionate care. I am a non-smoker and comfortable with pets.

name: Robert Jensen
bio: I am a retired registered nurse with 30 years of clinical experience across hospital, rehabilitation, and home health settings. I have recently returned to hands-on care work because I missed the connection with patients. I have extensive experience with dementia, Parkinson's, and post-stroke care. I am a non-smoker and I am comfortable with animals.
prior_experience: 15 years ICU and rehabilitation nursing at Regional Medical Center
10 years home health nursing
5 years care coordination for complex elderly cases

name: Fatima Hassan
bio: I have 6 years of experience in home-based elder care. I am gentle, patient, and I take great pride in the quality of care I provide. I am particularly good at building trust with clients who are initially hesitant or private. I am a non-smoker and I love animals — I volunteer at an animal shelter on weekends.
prior_experience: 6 years private home care across 4 client families
One client had mild cognitive impairment

name: Diana Patel
profile_completeness: partial — missing skills list and certifications
bio: I have spent most of my adult life caring for family members with serious health conditions, including my late mother who had Alzheimer's for 9 years. That experience gave me a depth of understanding that no training program can fully teach. I understand dementia from the inside. I am now offering my experience professionally. I am calm, patient, and deeply committed. Non-smoker. Very comfortable with dogs — I have two.
advanced_care_support: Alzheimer's — all stages based on personal caregiving experience
prior_experience: 9 years primary family caregiver for mother with Alzheimer's
Currently transitioning to professional caregiving

name: Susan Baker
bio: I love helping people and I have always been a caring person. I have worked with elderly people in the past and I am comfortable in home settings. I am reliable and hard working.
prior_experience: Some experience with elderly people

name: Tom Wilson
bio: I have several years of experience in caregiving and I am looking for morning shifts. I am good with elderly clients and I am a non-smoker.
prior_experience: Several years of caregiving experience

name: Maria Garcia
bio: I have some experience working with elderly people and I enjoy helping seniors stay comfortable at home. I am patient and caring. I can do light cleaning and cooking.
prior_experience: Helped care for a neighbour who was elderly

name: Kevin Brown
bio: I am recently retired and I want to give back. I have always enjoyed spending time with older people and I feel I have a lot to offer. I am patient, a good listener, and I am looking to help a family that needs support. I am a non-smoker.

schedule.hours: Flexible — I have no other commitments

name: Ashley Johnson
bio: I am passionate about helping others and I am eager to get into elder care. I am a quick learner and I am very energetic and friendly. I love meeting new people and I enjoy keeping busy.

name: Frank Miller
bio: I have some experience with caregiving from looking after my own parents in their later years. I am mature, calm, and I understand what elderly people go through. I am looking for part-time morning work. I am a non-smoker.
prior_experience: Cared for own parents informally
